name: Growth
tagline: Run experiments end to end with workflows that test, measure, and iterate on a schedule.
heroTitle: Ship more experiments, wait less for results.
heroSubtitle: Chain the whole growth loop into a workflow: generate variants, launch them, pull the numbers, and write the readout, on a schedule or a trigger. Agents connect to your analytics and channels so an experiment runs itself while you decide what to scale.
intro: Growth is throughput: more experiments, faster cycles, cleaner readouts. The bottleneck is rarely ideas, it's the manual glue between generating variants, launching them, and pulling results. Ryu turns that loop into a workflow that runs the repetitive parts automatically, so your team spends time on the calls that matter.

highlights.title: The loop as a workflow
highlights.description: Chain generate, launch, measure, and recap into one pipeline that runs on a schedule or a trigger.

highlights.title: Connected to your funnel
highlights.description: Agents reach your analytics, ad tools, and email platform to pull metrics and push changes directly.

highlights.title: Variants at volume
highlights.description: Spin up dozens of copy, subject-line, and creative variations grounded in what's already worked for you.

highlights.title: Cost-aware routing
highlights.description: The Gateway routes bulk generation to a cheap or local model and sets budgets so testing stays affordable.

useCases.title: Experiment design
useCases.description: Turn a hypothesis into a structured test: variants, success metric, sample size, and a stop rule, ready to launch.

useCases.title: Variant generation
useCases.description: Generate landing-page copy, ad creative angles, and subject lines at volume, grounded in your top performers.

useCases.title: Funnel analysis
useCases.description: Pull conversion data across the funnel, find the biggest drop-off, and propose the next experiment to run there.

useCases.title: Automated readouts
useCases.description: On a schedule, gather a running test's results and write a readout with significance and a recommendation.

useCases.title: Lifecycle and onboarding emails
useCases.description: Draft and test a full onboarding or re-engagement sequence, then queue it into your email platform.

useCases.title: Cohort and retention digging
useCases.description: Slice users into cohorts, compare retention curves, and summarize what separates the ones that stick.

examplePrompts: Design an A/B test for our signup page hero, with variants, the metric, and a stop rule.
Generate 20 ad headline variations based on our 5 best performers and group them by angle.
Find the biggest drop-off in our activation funnel and propose three experiments to fix it.
Every Monday, pull last week's running tests and write a readout with significance and a recommendation.
Compare 30-day retention across users who used feature X in week one versus those who didn't.

faq.q: Can a whole experiment run without me babysitting it?
faq.a: Yes. Build the loop as a workflow with schedules and triggers, so it generates variants, launches them through connected tools, pulls results, and drafts the readout. You step in to decide what to scale.

faq.q: Won't generating this many variants get expensive?
faq.a: The Gateway routes bulk generation to a cheap or fully local model and the high-stakes piece to a frontier one, with budgets per workflow. You control the spend and see every call.

faq.q: Can it act on our analytics and ad tools directly?
faq.a: With Connections, agents authenticate into your analytics, ad platforms, and email tools to pull metrics and push changes. You can keep a sign-off step before anything goes live.

ctaTitle: Turn your growth loop into a pipeline.
ctaSubtitle: Generate, launch, measure, and read out, automatically, so you can run more bets at once.
